name: Short Route Trail "El Valle y las Hectáreas" — circular from Alamillo
name_original: Sendero de Pequeño Recorrido «El Valle y las Hectáreas» — circular desde Alamillo
region_intro: Hiking around Alamillo offers diverse landscapes, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites. The region features a mix of natural terrain and areas shaped by past mining activities. Trails often lead through woodlands and open countryside, providing varied hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Alamillo The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Santa Eufemia and Miramontes Castle Loop, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Sierra de Santa Eufemia and passes by Miramontes Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the Castillo de Chillón — circular through the Parque Minero de Almadén, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This circular trail includes an ascent to Castillo de Chillón and traverses the historic Parque Minero de Almadén. Local hikers also love the Santa Eufemia — circular by the río Guadalmez, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Alamillo is defined by its river paths, historic castles, and varied mining landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alamillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Alamillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alamillo?
A: There are over a dozen hiking trails around Alamillo, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The komoot community has explored these routes over 20 times.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Alamillo?
A: Yes, Alamillo offers several easy hikes perfect for beginners or families. There are 4 routes classified as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively flat 3.1-mile (5.0 km) path that takes about 1 hour 19 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the region's landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes around Alamillo?
A: Hiking around Alamillo offers diverse landscapes, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites. Trails often lead through woodlands and open countryside, with areas shaped by past mining activities, providing varied hiking experiences.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alamillo?
A: Yes, many routes in Alamillo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197289964/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-chillon-circular-through-the-parque-minero-de-almaden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Chillón — circular through the Parque Minero de Almadén&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 6.2-mile (10.0 km) circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249126280/santa-eufemia-circular-by-the-rio-guadalmez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Eufemia — circular by the río Guadalmez&lt;/a&gt; is another great option.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Alamillo?
A: The region around Alamillo is rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Mining Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of a UNESCO World Heritage site, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de la Villa&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262987/sierra-de-santa-eufemia-and-miramontes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Santa Eufemia and Miramontes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by Miramontes Castle.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Alamillo?
A: Hikes in Alamillo vary in length and duration. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 1.5 hours, while longer, moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262987/sierra-de-santa-eufemia-and-miramontes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Santa Eufemia and Miramontes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; might take around 4.5 hours. There are also more challenging routes that can extend to 6 hours or more.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Many trails in Alamillo offer scenic views, particularly those traversing the rolling hills and river valleys. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197289964/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-chillon-circular-through-the-parque-minero-de-almaden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Chillón&lt;/a&gt; provide elevated perspectives over the landscape, including the historic Parque Minero de Almadén.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alamillo?
A: The best time to hike in Alamillo is generally during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable for hiking, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Alamillo?
A: The trails in Alamillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical elements like castles and mining parks, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Alamillo?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284099752/short-route-trail-el-valle-y-las-hectareas-circular-from-alamillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Short Route Trail "El Valle y las Hectáreas" — circular from Alamillo&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 13.5 miles (21.7 km) with significant elevation changes, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Alamillo?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, Alamillo is a rural area, and parking is generally available in or near the villages where trails begin. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot route details for specific starting points.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Alamillo and its surrounding villages offer local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals. While not directly on every trail, you can typically find cafes or restaurants in the main town centers, which are often starting or ending points for many hikes.
tips: The town is located to the west of the Alcudia valley. Its economic activity has been based on agriculture and livestock. It is surrounded by places with a high degree of natural conservation, with typical vegetation of the area: holm oak, mastic, sticky rockrose and labiernago.
To get to the viewpoint you have to deviate from the route, there are signs, so you won't miss it for sure. The route is recommended to do especially in spring and autumn.
